content.full_text:
Lewis J. Ferrari, Ph.D.
2205 Covillaud Street
Marysville, California 95901

December 29, 1990

Ms. Sandy Taylor
Political Consultant
California Fair Political Practices Commission,
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
SACRAMENTO, CA. 95804-0814

Dear Ms. Taylor:

This is a follow up to our telephone conversation of last week where I inquired about surplus funds involved in a political campaign. Specifically in my case when a balance of $103.73 is left over in my campaign account. Please see copy of bank statement enclosed.

FACTS INVOLVED:
I opened a bank account with First Interstate Bank, Marysville, CA. on August 8, 1990 with my own funds of $100.00.

This was a loan on my part because I did not intend to solicit funds from the public nor did I accept any contributions. Obviously, I did not know what costs I would have during the campaign and added personal funds as the campaign progressed and as needed. See copy of my Accounting and Record of Activity of Activity.

The election was held on November 6, 1990. I was not successful.

I now wish to comply with the requirements for terminating the election and file whatever papers are required.

In my call to you, you advised that I could terminate my campaign and election requirements if:
1. I filed Forms 501 and 502 and Form 415.
2. That the monies deposited to my account were personal and a loan.
3. The balance of $103.73 would be a pay back to me since it was a loan.

Enclosed you will find Forms 501 and 502 and 415 completed. Also included is a copy of my bank statement and Accounting and Record of Activity.

In trying to understand the volume of material handed to me for the election, I had some difficulty in the interpretation, so I conferred for information with Ms. Elisabeth "Liz" Ahart, Director of Administrative Services and City Clerk.
She referred to the Election Code Section 12400, read it to me and then advised that I have two options with the monies that were not spent in my campaign, (1) any surplus may be given to a charity, or (2) if I plan on running again, I could leave the account active with the surplus in it.

December 29, 1990

Is there any conflict with complying with Election Code Section 12400 and filing Forms 501, 502 and 415 for termination and pay off to me of the balance in the account?

I appreciate your assistance with this matter.

Please advise if I am in compliance with all of the requirements for closing and terminating my candidacy.

Sincerely,

Lewis J. Ferrari

cc
Ms. Elisabeth "Liz" Ahart, City Clerk.

Enclosed
1. Form 415-1990 FPPC
2. Form 502-FPPC
3. Form 501-FPPC
4. Bank Statement 11/8/90
5. Accounting and Record of Activity.
